title: Appendix-IV.-Procedure-for-Student-Code-of-Conduct-Incidents.pdf
content:
# Trang 1
27 
 
APPENDIX IV. PROCEDURE FOR STUDENT CODE OF CONDUCT INCIDENTS 
 
I.ABBREVIATIONS AND FORMS 
SAM: Student Affairs Management 
SDC: Student Disciplinary Committee 
Form 1 (FRM01): Suspected Misconduct Report Form 
Form 2 (FRM02): Student Misconduct Report Form 
 
Refer to Student Code of Conduct Policy and Procedure 
 
II. GENERAL FLOW FOR STUDENT CODE OF CONDUCT INCIDENTS 
Step 
Responsibility 
Workflow 
Time 
Form 
Details 
1 
Faculty members, 
university staff, 
students 
 
 
 
 
FRM01 
- Evidence can be soft copy and/or hard 
copy of the violated actions. Ex: photo, 
video, suspended misconduct report, etc. 
- Initial handling: when a misconduct action 
is found and required to take immediate 
action, such as using alcohol, drug, fighting, 
sexual harassement etc. If it is safe to do so, 
the faculty member, university staff, 
residential assistants can have initial 
handling before report the case to report 
channels. 
2 
SAM 
 
 
Within 3 working 
days since the 
violation date 
 
- SAM notifies the student via email (and 
through an in-person meeting if desired) that 
an offense is under investigation process. 
Skip the email notification if there is no need 
more investiagation and the sanction is 
already announced when case happens. 
- Student can request a Hearing at this stage 
if they feel it can support their situation. 
Suspect 
violations report 
via email or 
phone call 
 
 
Initial 
handling (if 
needed) 
 
Investiagate/Collect more 
evidence (if any)

# Trang 2
28 
 
Step 
Responsibility 
Workflow 
Time 
Form 
Details 
3 
SAM 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
- SAM checks whether the student has 
previously violated Student Code of 
Conduct. 
4 
SAM 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Within 3 working 
days since the 
violation date 
 
- SAM follows up on the case in 
consultation with other units if needed and 
suggests the appropriate penalty/sanction 
based on the set of guidelines determined at 
the university level. 
- Students may be asked for a meeting with 
the Committee to explain and self-justify the 
situation. 
5 
SAM (Level 1, 2) 
SDC (Level 3, 4) 
 
 
 
 
Within 3 working 
days since the 
violation date 
 
- SAM determines the resolution and 
sanctions on the case at level 1 and level 2. 
- From level 3 and level 4, SAM shall bring 
the case to SDC for discussion and making 
decision. 
- Meeting with students can be required 
during this stage. 
6 
SAM 
 
 
 
 
Within 5 working 
days since the 
violation date 
 
- A formal letter is sent out from 
conduct@vinuni.edu.vn and a meeting with 
student to inform the decision for level 3 and 
level 4. 
- If the decision is a suspension or dismissal, 
the Committee shall seek the Provost or 
Determine resolution 
(learning tasks or sanctions) 
 
Notify the student of decision 
Yes 
Confirm 
violation 
The matter 
is closed 
No 
Define the severity of student 
misconduct 
Level 1 
Level 2 
Level 3 
Level 4

# Trang 3
29 
 
Step 
Responsibility 
Workflow 
Time 
Form 
Details 
President’s approval and students' parents 
will be informed. 
7 
Student 
 
 
 
Appeal within 10 working days after the 
decision is announced 
8 
Student 
 
 
FRM02 
 
9 
SAM 
 
 
 
- All allegations of misconduct and the 
outcomes must be documented and archived 
at SAM Office. 
- The information will be entered into the 
Student Code of Conduct database. 
 
 
 
Yes 
Accept 
decision 
Signed Violation 
Report Form 
No 
Appeal process 
Close-out and 
Record Keeping

- Initial handling: when a misconduct action

President’s approval and students' parents

Appeal within 10 working days after the

- All allegations of misconduct and the
outcomes must be documented and archived
at SAM Office.
- The information will be entered into the
Student Code of Conduct database.
